 {"id":"bd-dqmd","title":"Harden cleanup_review_worktrees against corrupted mapping.json paths","description":"Problem: cleanup_review_worktrees() reads worktree paths from the state mapping file (mapping.json) and may fall back to direct 'rm -rf' deletion when git worktree removal fails. mapping.json is an untrusted state file that can be corrupted/tampered; without validation that wt_path is under the expected worktrees run directory, ru could delete an arbitrary directory.

Root cause: unsafe deletion uses paths from state files without verifying they are inside the intended base directory.

Fix:
- Add a helper to validate that a candidate path is a subdirectory of the run worktrees base (canonicalized).
- Refuse to delete wt_path unless it is under base.
- Also validate base is under the ru worktrees root before final rm -rf.
- Add a unit regression test that seeds mapping.json with an outside path and asserts cleanup does not remove it.

Acceptance:
- cleanup_review_worktrees never deletes directories outside its computed base.
- Unit test covers the regression.
- ShellCheck warning+ passes.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-05T15:35:23.220261569-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T15:37:46.83606423-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-05T15:37:46.83606423-05:00","close_reason":"Added _is_path_under_base guard and run_id validation to prevent cleanup_review_worktrees from rm -rf outside its run directory; added unit regression test in scripts/test_unit_worktree.sh; ShellCheck + bash -n pass."}
 {"id":"bd-drb5","title":"Real unit tests for config management","description":"Test config management using real file operations.

Functions to test:
- get_config_value(): Read config values
- set_config_value(): Write config values
- is_valid_config_key(): Validate config keys
- ensure_config_exists(): Create default config
- resolve_config(): Merge config sources

Test cases:
- XDG config dir creation
- Config file read/write
- Default value handling
- Environment variable overrides
- Invalid config handling

Uses create_test_env() for isolated XDG dirs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:53:35.249856744-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T10:55:53.355219209-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-05T10:55:53.355219209-05:00","close_reason":"Added 16 tests for is_valid_config_key (11 tests) and resolve_config (5 tests). Total test file now has 35 tests covering all config functions: get_config_value, set_config_value, ensure_config_exists, is_valid_config_key, and resolve_config.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-drb5","depends_on_id":"bd-fudb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:53:35.269986049-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-e1eo","title":"Epic: Comprehensive Test Coverage Without Mocks","description":"# Epic: Comprehensive Test Coverage Without Mocks

## Overview
This epic establishes complete test coverage for the repo_updater (ru) project using real implementations instead of mocks. The goal is 90%+ function coverage with detailed logging and full E2E integration tests.

## Current State Analysis
- **Total functions in ru**: 316
- **Functions with unit tests**: 82 (~26%)
- **Functions using mocks**: Most existing tests use function overrides as mocks
- **E2E coverage**: Partial, inconsistent logging

## Target State
- **Unit test coverage**: 90%+ without mocks (real function calls)
- **E2E test coverage**: All major workflows with structured JSON logging
- **Test isolation**: Each test runs in isolated temp directory
- **Logging**: Machine-readable JSON logs with timing, context, and results

## Sub-Epics (5 total)

### 1. Test Framework Enhancement (bd-wrfp)
Enhance test_framework.sh with JSON logging, coverage tracking, and parallel execution.

### 2. Core Functions Unit Tests (bd-fudb)
Unit tests for: URL parsing, git operations, configuration, repo list management, worktree helpers, JSON utilities.

### 3. Review Feature Unit Tests (bd-c3vu)
Unit tests for the review feature (bd-4bmq): state management, locking, discovery, validation, quality gates, gh_actions, metrics.

### 4. Driver Function Tests (bd-68rr)
Unit tests for: local driver, driver interface, rate limit governor.

### 5. E2E Integration Tests (bd-6crg)
Complete workflows: sync, review, clone, worktree, config, error handling. All with comprehensive JSON logging.

## Dependencies
All sub-epics depend on this parent epic. Within E2E tests, the logging infrastructure (bd-g7gw) must be completed first.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] bd stats shows 90%+ function coverage
- [ ] All tests pass without mocks
- [ ] JSON logs capture full audit trail
- [ ] CI pipeline integrates all tests
- [ ] Test execution time < 5 minutes total","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:52:18.285415667-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T22:55:30.999144851-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T22:55:30.999144851-05:00","close_reason":"Epic planning complete: Created 5 sub-epics and 27 tasks with full dependency structure. Test coverage target: 90%+ without mocks. Implementation work tracked in sub-epics bd-wrfp, bd-fudb, bd-c3vu, bd-68rr, bd-6crg."}
